mappings.rationaleAI: The Mughal Empire from Babur's invasion through Akbar's consolidation, Shah Jahan's architectural achievements, Aurangzeb's expansion, and the empire's decline directly correspond to the entire section C on Mughal unification and administration of the subcontinent (1526-1761).

The Sikh Empire under Ranjit Singh (1799-1849) is chronologically later than this section's main focus, but provides context for the Maratha and post-Mughal period dynamics; however, the section ends at 1761 so the overlap is limited to the broader religious and political context.

The Indian Mutiny of 1857 is chronologically beyond this section but the episode covers the dissolution of the Mughal dynasty and British takeover, providing end-context for C.4 on Mughal decline culminating in foreign invasions (1731-61).
